source.multipart_basis: Decomposition mirrors a co-molded ratcheting-screwdriver handle: ABS structural core, TPE Shore A 65 grip overmold, distal ratchet housing, internal pawl, internal ratchet gear, shaft / spindle, 1/4-inch hex bit holder, and rear end cap.

full_prompt:
Design a redesigned injection-molded ratcheting screwdriver body as an open-source consumer-product DFM benchmark, targeting a consumer hardware-store DIY segment. The screwdriver body consists of a handle (150 mm long x 35 mm max diameter), a ratcheting mechanism housing, and a 6.35 mm (1/4 inch) hex bit holder at the distal end. The handle is co-molded with a hard ABS (yield 45 MPa, E 2.3 GPa, density 1050 kg/m^3) core and a TPE grip over-mold (Shore A 65). Internal ratcheting mechanism is a metal pawl + gear assembly and is treated as rigid in this competition (designer-specified off-the-shelf). The tool must deliver up to 5 N-m of torque to the driver bit with a maximum user grip force of 100 N applied around the TPE grip. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) max von Mises stress in the ABS core when 5 N-m torque is applied at the bit with the user gripping at 100 N axial + 20 N-m grip moment must not exceed 30 MPa (ABS yield 45 / SF 1.5), (R2) handle twist (angular deflection between grip and bit) under 5 N-m torque must not exceed 2 degrees to preserve driving feel, (R3) drop test from 1 m onto a hard surface (equivalent 250 N quasi-static impulse) must not crack the ABS core, (R4) handle part must demold with a single line of draw with a minimum draft angle of 1 degree on all vertical faces per DFM best practice, and (R5) total handle assembly mass (ABS core + TPE overmold, excluding metal mechanism) must not exceed 80 g. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with ABS core, TPE grip, and bit holder as sub-bodies plus a verification report listing max von Mises, handle twist angle, drop result, DFM draft check, and total mass.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This design is delivered as a multi-part assembly rather than a single solid body. The decomposition comprises (A) abs_handle_core, (B) tpe_grip_overmold, (C) ratchet_housing, (D) ratchet_gear, (E) pawl, (F) drive_spindle, (G) hex_bit_holder, (H) rear_end_cap. The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design a redesigned injection-molded ratcheting screwdriver body as an open-source consumer-product DFM benchmark.

The body consists of a handle (150 mm long x 35 mm max diameter), a ratcheting mechanism housing, and a 6.35 mm (1/4 inch) hex bit holder at the distal end. The handle is co-molded with a hard ABS (yield 45 MPa, E 2.3 GPa, density 1050 kg/m^3) core and a TPE grip over-mold (Shore A 65). Internal ratcheting mechanism is a metal pawl + gear assembly treated as rigid.

The tool must deliver up to 5 N-m of torque to the driver bit with a maximum user grip force of 100 N applied around the TPE grip.

Load cases: LC1 max torque 5 N-m applied at the bit with the user gripping at 100 N axial + 20 N-m grip moment; LC2 drop test from 1 m onto a hard surface (equivalent 250 N quasi-static impulse).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Draft angles, wall thickness uniformity, and parting-line checks are a DFM rule-set, not FEA.

prompt.title: Ratcheting screwdriver handle DFM benchmark (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a co-molded ABS + TPE ratcheting screwdriver handle (150 mm x 35 mm) driving 5 N-m torque to a 1/4-inch bit, passing ABS stress, twist, drop, 1-degree DFM draft, and 80 g mass. [Multi-part assembly] abs_handle_core (x1), tpe_grip_overmold (x1), ratchet_housing (x1), ratchet_gear (x1), pawl (x1), drive_spindle (x1), hex_bit_holder (x1), rear_end_cap (x1). FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

parts.name: abs_handle_core
parts.role: structural ABS core carrying torque from grip to ratchet housing; primary stress body for R1 and R2
parts.material_hint: ABS, yield 45 MPa, E 2.3 GPa, density 1050 kg/m^3, injection molded with 1 deg draft

parts.name: tpe_grip_overmold
parts.role: Shore A 65 TPE grip overmolded on ABS core for user grip traction and distributed load
parts.material_hint: TPE Shore A 65, co-molded with chemical bond to ABS substrate

parts.name: ratchet_housing
parts.role: distal metal housing containing ratchet gear + pawl; provides rigid bearing for spindle
parts.material_hint: Zinc die-cast or steel housing, yield >= 200 MPa

parts.name: ratchet_gear
parts.role: internal toothed gear engaged by pawl; transmits torque in drive direction, freewheels in reverse

parts.name: pawl
parts.role: spring-loaded pawl engaging ratchet gear D; switch selects direction
parts.material_hint: Hardened steel pawl with torsion spring

parts.name: drive_spindle
parts.role: steel shaft coaxial with handle, rigidly coupled to ratchet gear and hex bit holder; transmits 5 N-m

parts.name: hex_bit_holder
parts.role: 1/4-inch hex socket at distal end with magnetic or ball-detent retention for bits
parts.material_hint: Hardened steel with ball detent, Rc >= 55

parts.name: rear_end_cap
parts.role: rear closure sealing the handle cavity and providing palm surface
parts.material_hint: ABS end cap, snap-fit into core A

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection DCR across all inter-part interfaces (co-mold bond, press-fit, snap-fit, gear-pawl engagement) <= 1.0 under LC1 5 N-m torque.
ABS-core to ratchet-housing press-fit interface must transfer full 5 N-m without slip; min static friction torque margin >= 1.5.

interfaces.load_path: TPE grip B is chemically bonded to ABS core A during two-shot molding; transmits distributed grip torque into core
ratchet housing C is press-fit + retained by internal ribs in ABS core A; transfers 5 N-m reaction torque
ratchet gear D rotates on bearing bore inside housing C; bearing reaction is contact-mediated
pawl E engages gear D tooth; load transfer by tooth-contact force during drive direction
drive spindle F is press-fit / keyed into ratchet gear D; transmits full output torque
hex bit holder G is press-fit onto spindle F with retaining pin; delivers 5 N-m to bit
rear end cap H snap-fits into ABS core A, enclosing cavity; minimal load path

fem_check_policy.rationale: Handle twist, torque transfer through the co-molded ABS/TPE and press-fit ratchet housing, and drop integrity can only be validated on the assembled model; per-part metrics are reported but do not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A ratcheting screwdriver is a two-shot plastic handle plus a steel ratchet mechanism, bit holder, and rear cap — inherently multi-body.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] counts and interfaces[] reflect typical ratcheting-screwdriver architecture but are speculative; not derived from a specific PTC Creo competition brief.
